title: Smoky Fluffy Argentinian Fish Empanadas
description: A crowd-pleasing Argentinian dish featuring red pepper flakes and deglazed onions, perfect for a pescatarian dinner.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher or sea salt for best flavor

ingredients.name: Active Dry Yeast
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality yeast for best rise

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Use a mild, fruity olive oil

ingredients.name: Egg, beaten
ingredients.notes: For egg wash and empanada filling

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: For egg wash and empanada dough

instructions: In a small bowl, combine yeast, sugar, and 1/4 cup warm water. Let sit for 5-10 minutes, until frothy.
In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and red pepper flakes.
Add yeast mixture, butter, olive oil, and egg to dry ingredients. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead dough for 5-7 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Let dough rise in a warm, draft-free place for 1-2 hours, until doubled in size.
Meanwhile, caramelize onion in butter until dark golden brown.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add cod pieces and cook for 2-3 minutes, until opaque.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Roll out dough to 1/4 inch thickness. Use a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles.
Place a tablespoon of cod mixture onto one half of each circle. Fold dough in half and press edges to seal.
Brush tops with egg wash and bake for 15-20 minutes, until golden brown.
